fallacies.name: Straw Man
fallacies.description: Misrepresenting an opponent's position to make it easier to attack.
fallacies.example: So you're saying we should just let total chaos reign? Bold.
fallacies.counter: I never claimed that — here's what I actually said.

fallacies.name: Ad Hominem
fallacies.description: Attacking the person instead of their argument.
fallacies.example: You'd believe anything — you failed logic class, remember?
fallacies.counter: My grades aren't the claim on the table. Address the claim.

fallacies.name: Slippery Slope
fallacies.description: Asserting one small step inevitably leads to a catastrophic chain.
fallacies.example: Allow this once and civilization itself unravels by Tuesday.
fallacies.counter: Name the mechanism. Each step needs its own evidence.

fallacies.name: False Dilemma
fallacies.description: Framing a many-optioned question as only two choices.
fallacies.example: Either you agree with me completely, or you're the enemy.
fallacies.counter: That's a false choice — there's a whole spectrum between.
